reasoning: France have emerged as the clear market favorite at roughly +400 after two dominant wins (3-1 Senegal, 3-0 Iraq) with Mbappé in red-hot form, backed by 2018-winning pedigree and elite attacking depth in Cherki, Barcola, Olise and Dembélé. Spain remain the second choice with the highest ceiling thanks to Yamal and Pedri, recovering from a shock Cape Verde draw with a 4-0 rout of Saudi Arabia, while defending champions Argentina (Messi scoring all five of their goals) and England are the next threats. The main risks to France are the brutal expanded 48-team knockout path and injury attrition among frontrunners, plus the altitude/travel quirks across the tri-nation host setup. Morocco is the standout dark horse, surging on the odds board after a competitive draw with Brazil and a win over Scotland, carrying genuine knockout pedigree from their 2022 semifinal run.
